     1|---
     2|title: "Phage-Host Prediction Analysis"
     3|params:
     4|  name: "02-phage-host-prediction"
     5|---
     6|
     7|# Phage-Host Prediction for Animal Pathogens
     8|
     9|## Setup
    10|
    11|```{r setup}
    12|#| label: setup
    13|here::i_am(paste0(params$name, ".qmd"), uuid = "b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8901-bcde-f1234567890")
    14|
    15|# qproj setup
    16|qproj::proj_create_dir_target(params$name)
    17|path_target <- qproj::path_target(params$name)
    18|path_source <- qproj::path_source(params$name)
    19|
    20|# Read upstream data
    21|path_upstream <- qproj::path_source("01-public-data-mining")
    22|```
    23|
    24|## Load Packages
    25|
    26|```{r packages}
    27|#| label: packages
    28|library(tidyverse)
    29|library(here)
    30|library(qproj)
    31|library(ape)
    32|library(ggplot2)
    33|# For phage-host prediction
    34|# library(phageR)      # If available
    35|# library(vConTACT2R)  # If available
    36|```
    37|
    38|## 1. Objective
    39|
    40|Predict which phages can effectively target specific animal pathogens:
    41|- **Target pathogens**: *Clostridium perfringens*, *Salmonella enterica*, *E. coli*, *Vibrio* spp.
    42|- **Goal**: Identify lytic phages with broad host range or specific activity
    43|- **Approach**: Use computational tools + existing databases (phagesDB, MVP, vConTACT3)
    44|
    45|## 2. Phage-Host Prediction Tools Review
    46|
    47|Based on NotebookLM research (34 sources imported), key tools include:
    48|
    49|### 2.1 Sequence-Based Methods
    50|- **vConTACT3**: Viral protein cluster networks, genus-level prediction
    51|- **PHIAF**: Phage-host interaction predictor using k-mer features
    52|- **DeepHost**: Deep learning approach using genomic features
    53|- **WIsH**: k-mer based method
    54|
    55|### 2.2 Structure-Based Methods
    56|- **PHP**: Prokaryotic host prediction using structural features
    57|- **VHQS**: Quality scoring for predicted interactions
    58|
    59|### 2.3 Integrated Pipelines
    60|- **ViroProfiler** (our existing tool)
    61|- **TPD** (Therapeutic Phage Discovery pipeline)
    62|
    63|## 3. Database Integration
    64|
    65|### 3.1 Download Phage Genomes from RefSeq
    66|
    67|Template for downloading phage genomes targeting our pathogens of interest.
    68|
    69|```{r download-phage-genomes}
    70|#| label: download-phage-genomes
    71|#| eval: false
    72|
    73|# Using entrez to download phage genomes
    74|if (FALSE) {  # Set to TRUE when ready
    75|  library(rentrez)
    76|  
    77|  # Search for phages targeting C. perfringens
    78|  search_cperf <- rentrez::entrez_search(
    79|    db = "nucleotide",
    80|    term = "Clostridium perfringens phage[Organism] AND refseq[filter]",
    81|    retmax = 100
    82|  )
    83|  
    84|  # Download sequences
    85|  phage_seqs <- rentrez::entrez_fetch(
    86|    db = "nucleotide",
    87|    id = search_cperf$ids,
    88|    rettype = "fasta"
    89|  )
    90|  
    91|  # Save
    92|  writeLines(
    93|    phage_seqs,
    94|    file.path(path_target, "raw", "c_perfringens_phages.fasta")
    95|  )
    96|}
    97|```
    98|
    99|### 3.2 Load Existing Database
   100|
   101|Load phage-host interaction data from integrated databases.
   102|
   103|```{r load-databases}
   104|#| label: load-databases
   105|#| eval: false
   106|
   107|# Template: Load phagesDB data
   108|# Source: https://phagesdb.org/download/
   109|phagesdb_url <- "https://phagesdb.org/media/ExportDatasets/"
   110|
   111|# Template: Load vConTACT3 results if available
   112|# vcontact_results <- read_tsv(file.path(path_upstream, "processed", "vcontact_results.tsv"))
   113|
   114|message("Database loading templates ready")
   115|```
   116|
   117|## 4. Host Prediction Analysis
   118|
   119|### 4.1 Using ViroProfiler Pipeline
   120|
   121|Our existing ViroProfiler pipeline can be used for phage-host prediction.
   122|
   123|```{r viroprofiler-template}
   124|#| label: viroprofiler-template
   125|#| eval: false
   126|
   127|# Template for running ViroProfiler
   128|# ViroProfiler path (update as needed)
   129|viroprofiler_path <- "~/tools/ViroProfiler"
   130|
   131|# Command template
   132|viroprofiler_cmd <- sprintf(
   133|  "cd %s && bash run_viroprofiler.sh -i %s -o %s -t phage_host",
   134|  viroprofiler_path,
   135|  file.path(path_upstream, "raw", "phage_contigs.fasta"),
   136|  file.path(path_target, "processed", "viroprofiler_output")
   137|)
   138|
   139|# Execute (when ready)
   140|# system(viroprofiler_cmd)
   141|```
   142|
   143|### 4.2 Using vConTACT3
   144|
   145|vConTACT3 for viral cluster-based host prediction.
   146|
   147|```{r vcontact-template}
   148|#| label: vcontact-template
   149|#| eval: false
   150|
   151|# Template for vConTACT3 analysis
   152|# Input: viral protein files
   153|# Output: cluster network with host predictions
   154|
   155|vcontact_cmd <- sprintf(
   156|  "vcontact3 --input-file %s --output-dir %s --threads 8",
   157|  file.path(path_upstream, "processed", "viral_proteins.faa"),
   158|  file.path(path_target, "processed", "vcontact3_output")
   159|)
   160|
   161|# Execute (when ready)
   162|# system(vcontact_cmd)
   163|```
   164|
   165|## 5. Results Integration
   166|
   167|### 5.1 Parse Prediction Results
   168|
   169|```{r parse-results}
   170|#| label: parse-results
   171|#| eval: false
   172|
   173|# Template: Parse ViroProfiler/vConTACT3 results
   174|# results_df <- read_tsv(file.path(path_target, "processed", "predictions.tsv"))
   175|
   176|# Summary table template
   177|# summary_table <- results_df %>%
   178|#   group_by(target_pathogen, phage_id) %>%
   179|#   summarise(
   180|#     prediction_score = mean(score),
   181|#     n_tools = n_distinct(tool),
   182|#     .groups = "drop"
   183|#   ) %>%
   184|#   arrange(desc(prediction_score))
   185|
   186|# knitr::kable(summary_table, caption = "Phage-Host Prediction Results")
   187|```
   188|
   189|### 5.2 Visualization
   190|
   191|```{r visualize-predictions}
   192|#| label: visualize-predictions
   193|#| eval: false
   194|
   195|# Template: Visualize prediction results
   196|# ggplot(summary_table, aes(x = target_pathogen, y = prediction_score, fill = n_tools)) +
   197|#   geom_boxplot() +
   198|#   theme_minimal() +
   199|#   labs(title = "Phage-Host Prediction Scores by Pathogen",
   200|#        x = "Target Pathogen", y = "Prediction Score")
   201|```
   202|
   203|## 6. Candidate Phage Selection
   204|
   205|### 6.1 Selection Criteria
   206|
   207|Based on the Research Master Plan:
   208|1. **Lytic lifestyle** (not temperate)
   209|2. **Broad host range** (target multiple strains)
   210|3. **Genomic stability** (few lysogenic genes)
   211|4. **Safety profile** (no toxin genes, no AMR genes)
   212|
   213|### 6.2 Top Candidates Table
   214|
   215|*To be populated after running predictions*
   216|
   217|| Phage ID | Target Pathogen | Prediction Score | Lifestyle | Host Range | Notes |
   218||-----------|-----------------|------------------|------------|-------------|-------|
   219|| TBD | C. perfringens | - | lytic | - | - |
   220|| TBD | S. enterica | - | lytic | - | - |
   221|| TBD | E. coli | - | lytic | - | - |
   222|| TBD | Vibrio spp. | - | lytic | - | - |
   223|
   224|## 7. NotebookLM Integration
   225|
   226|### 7.1 Query NotebookLM for Validation
   227|
   228|Use NotebookLM to validate predictions against literature.
   229|
   230|```bash
   231|nlm notebook query phage-synergy "What phages are effective against Clostridium perfringens in poultry?"
   232|```
   233|
   234|### 7.2 Add Results to NotebookLM
   235|
   236|After generating prediction results, add them as a source to NotebookLM for cross-referencing with literature.
   237|
   238|```bash
   239|# Convert results to text/PDF and add to NotebookLM
   240|nlm source add phage-synergy --text "$(cat predictions_summary.txt)" --title "Phage-Host Predictions pr0117"
   241|```
   242|
   243|## 8. Session Info
   244|
   245|```{r session-info}
   246|#| label: session-info
   247|sessionInfo()
   248|```
   249|
   250|## 9. Next Steps
   251|
   252|1. **Run ViroProfiler** on target phage genomes
   253|2. **Run vConTACT3** for cluster-based predictions
   254|3. **Integrate results** from multiple tools
   255|4. **Validate predictions** using NotebookLM literature
   256|5. **Proceed to Module 03** (`03-probiotic-target-identification.qmd`)
   257|
   258|---
   259|*Generated by qproj workflow for pr0117-PhageProbioticSynergy*
   260|
